extraction.one_line_summary: BAÑULS Tsgabu won via solo attack in final 15km after SUNWEB orchestrated dual breakaway infiltration with KELDERMAN Nicholas pacing KRAGH ANDERSON Søren.
tactical_patterns: SUNWEB planted KELDERMAN in early break then sent KRAGH ANDERSON bridging mid-race to engineer numerical advantage for GC gains.
BAÑULS attacked solo final 15km while MATTHEWS defended behind for 40 seconds, converting team infiltration into stage win.
Intermediate sprint placed before penultimate climb neutralized sprinter interest, allowing breakaway specialists to contest without peloton chase pressure.
SCHMANN crashed final kilometer but retained yellow as commissaires applied same-time rule despite self-inflicted mechanical, not genuine equipment failure.

rider_signals.KRAGH ANDERSON Søren: positive: bridged to break mid-race specifically to pace climbs, revealing SUNWEB's willingness to sacrifice GC podium position for stage control
rider_signals.BAÑULS Tsgabu: positive: soloed final 15km for stage win and moved to 18 seconds off yellow, showing unexpected GC threat capability
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: defended behind solo BAÑULS for 40 seconds then sprinted second, demonstrating diesel engine domestique value for GC protection
rider_signals.SCHMANN Maximilian: negative: overcook corner crash final kilometer exposed concentration lapse when defending jersey, retained yellow only via commissaire generosity
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: neutral: infiltrated early break but dropped when SUNWEB accelerated climbs, suggesting opportunistic rather than form-based break selection
